title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 11, 2026
headline: Oracle's WARN Act deadline passed with ~30,000 terminations executed, yet the Washington and Missouri investigations closed without a ruling — executed-but-unadjudicated, the boundary hold at 20.6 (0.6pt above Critical) is the sharpest unresolved carry-forward in the cycle; four sovereign floors reinforced simultaneously as Myanmar's cluster-munition school attack, Sudan's RSF terrorist designation and 28.9M food-insecure, Israel's ceasefire-violation systematicity, and North Korea's transnational forced labor reaching EU supply chains each deepen the active-perpetration record without moving the absolute floor.
summary: Tonight's cycle assessed nineteen entities across four indexes — fourteen priority, five rotation — and confirmed every published score without generating a new scored proposal. Three domains dominate the analytical record. First, the Oracle WARN boundary ruling: the June 15 sign-or-forfeit deadline has now passed and approximately 30,000 terminations are executed, but the Washington state and Missouri WARN-Act investigations closed with no adverse ruling or settlement as of the June 11 web check. A new methodology ruling — WARN-HELD-POST-DEADLINE-BUT-STILL-PRE-ADJUDICATION — establishes that executed terminations without an adverse adjudication do not verify the WARN-avoidance theory; Oracle held at boundary 20.6, the sharpest corporate carry-forward in the current cycle sequence. Second, floor reinforcement across four sovereigns: Israel (218 of 243 ceasefire days with attacks, 36% of allocated aid entering, 70%-seizure plan advancing), Myanmar (June 9 cluster-munition school attack in Karen State, 6 killed, June 9 airstrike on Karenni youth leader, June 4 hospital hit in Kachin), Sudan (RSF designated terrorists, 28.9M acutely food insecure, el-Fasher detention killings per Foreign Policy June 11), and North Korea (100,000+ in transnational state forced labor across 40 countries, wages remitted to the nuclear program, now documented in EU supply chains). All four floors were fully constituted in prior cycles; June 11 evidence deepens the accountability record without moving the absolute 0.0 designation. Third, Ebola Bundibugyo acceleration: DRC reaches 635 confirmed cases and 127 deaths — a +37 single-day case increase that is among the highest single-cycle jumps in the outbreak timeline — with 18 Ituri health zones now affected. Uganda's cross-border importation into Kampala and Wakiso continues with WHO/MOH contact-tracing active; community transmission not yet ruled out. The natural-disaster-overlay holds for both entities; documented state obstruction remains the conversion trigger through the July peak window. Anthropic's June 11 governance cluster — Amodei push for authority to block unsafe models, $350M pledge, Advanced AI Framework, IPO S-1 at approximately $965B — resolves as net-neutral: prospective governance posture and corporate-structure events are not operative conduct changes under the methodology. Confirmed at 59.1 Functional. Five rotation entities (Moldova, Tanzania, Sierra Leone, Dominican Republic, Peru) confirmed stable at 35.9 Developing on scanner evidence_found:false over the 14-day lookback.

topSignals.title: Oracle Corporation 20.6 — WARN Deadline Passed; ~30,000 Terminations Executed; WA/MO Investigations Closed Without Ruling; Hold Maintained
topSignals.description: The June 15 WARN Act sign-or-forfeit deadline has passed and approximately 30,000 terminations are now executed: affected workers were required to sign legal releases waiving the right to sue or forfeit their severance package, with Oracle absorbing the 60-day WARN pay period into severance rather than providing notice. RSUs forfeited without acceleration; H-1B visa holders, cancer patients, pregnant employees, and veterans were disproportionately represented among those excluded from enhanced severance. The Washington state and Missouri WARN-Act investigations — both examining the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory — closed as of the June 11 web check with no public resolution, no AG ruling, and no settlement. A new methodology ruling — WARN-HELD-POST-DEADLINE-BUT-STILL-PRE-ADJUDICATION — establishes that execution of terminations without an adverse adjudication does not verify the un-priced WARN-avoidance harm; the 60-day-as-severance structuring remains a disputed-but-unadjudicated compliance interpretation. Oracle held at boundary 20.6, 0.6 points above Critical. The Washington state class action remains an active civil adjudication channel; an adverse ruling constitutes the immediate crossing trigger.

topSignals.title: Israel 0.0 — 218 of 243 Ceasefire Days With Attacks; 36% Aid Entry; 70%-Seizure Plan Advancing; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: Israel's active-perpetration floor at 0.0 absorbs three concurrent reinforcement signals this cycle: 218 of the 243 days since the ceasefire agreement have included documented military attacks, establishing an attack-during-ceasefire systematicity record; only 36% of allocated humanitarian aid trucks have entered Gaza, with Oxfam documenting more than 100 consecutive days of water deprivation; and the 70%-of-Gaza seizure plan — covering approximately 40 documented military outpost positions, 8 established post-ceasefire in violation of the October 2025 agreement — continues to advance, adding a structural-permanence marker to the episodic-military-operation record. The 3,076+ documented ceasefire violations constitute the most granular systematicity record in the current assessment universe for any floor-designated entity. UAE external complicity (Sudan/RSF arming) and Microsoft Azure/Unit 8200 external complicity are scored under those actors under no-double-count discipline and not against this sovereign floor. The floor cannot move lower.

topSignals.title: Myanmar 0.0 — June 9 Cluster-Munition School Attack (Karen State, 6 Killed); Karenni Youth Leader Airstrike; Kachin Hospital Hit; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: Three acute June 9 events deepen Myanmar's active-perpetration floor this cycle: a cluster-munition attack on a school in Karen State killed 6 people and constitutes a documented attack on protected civilian educational infrastructure using munitions prohibited under the Convention on Cluster Munitions; an airstrike killed a Karenni youth leader in a targeted killing consistent with the junta's documented pattern of eliminating civil society leadership; and a June 4 airstrike hit a hospital in Kachin State, adding a fourth geographic theater to the multi-region active-perpetration record. With 5.2 million displaced, the Myanmar junta's systematic targeting of protected sites — now spanning schools, hospitals, religious sites (Bago monastery, June 10), and residential infrastructure — constitutes the broadest documented civilian-target-category record in the current assessment universe for a non-state-conflict actor. The floor is fully constituted at 0.0; additional documented target categories deepen the accountability record without moving the designation.

topSignals.title: Sudan 0.0 — RSF Designated Terrorists; 28.9M Acutely Food Insecure; El-Fasher Detention Killings; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: Sudan's active-perpetration floor absorbs three concurrent reinforcement signals: the RSF terrorist designation creates a new legal accountability dimension added to the existing UN Fact-Finding Mission genocide-characteristics finding and IPC Phase 5 famine designation for 2 million; Foreign Policy's June 11 documentation of ethnically-motivated detention killings in el-Fasher adds a new mass-atrocity event to the Darfur record; and the 28.9 million acutely food insecure — with the $2.8B humanitarian appeal only 40% funded — represents the most severe compound humanitarian emergency in the current assessment universe in absolute scale. UAE complicity through RSF arming is scored under UAE and not double-counted against this sovereign floor. SAF and RSF systematic attacks on hospitals and displacement camps, combined with the humanitarian-access obstruction that enabled the famine designation, fully constitute the active-perpetration multi-category threshold. The floor cannot move lower.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 — Ebola Bundibugyo 635/127; +37 Cases in One Day; 18 Ituri Health Zones; Near-Floor Confirmed
topSignals.description: The DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak has reached 635 confirmed cases and 127 deaths — a single-day increase of 37 cases that is among the highest single-cycle jumps in the outbreak timeline. Eighteen of 36 Ituri health zones are now affected, expanding from the 17 documented in the prior cycle. No approved vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain. Contact-tracing covers approximately 20% of known transmission chains. The concurrent humanitarian burden — M23/AFC conflict in eastern DRC, 26.5 million food-insecure, world's largest displacement crisis at 6.9 million — compounds the epidemiological shock. The natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count hold at near-floor 2.3 remains intact: the primary harm driver is an exogenous epidemiological event and both WHO and DRC government cooperation with the PHEIC response has been documented. Deliberate documented state obstruction of the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — remains the only conversion trigger. The July peak window is the structural review deadline.

topSignals.title: North Korea 0.0 — 100,000+ in Transnational State Forced Labor Across 40 Countries; EU Supply Chains Documented; Floor Reinforced
topSignals.description: North Korea's state-organized transnational forced labor program — 100,000+ workers deployed across 40 countries with confiscated passports, 12–16 hour days, 364 days per year, wages remitted directly to the nuclear weapons program — is now documented as reaching EU manufacturing supply chains. The EU high-risk-database obligation due June 14 is the nearest hard deadline on the importer-side enforcement axis. The DPRK floor is fully constituted at 0.0 by the domestic systematic-oppression record (political prison camps, state surveillance apparatus, execution of those attempting to leave); the forced-labor evidence extends the floor's reach across borders but cannot move the DPRK sovereign score lower. The scorable EU-importer and Fortune 500 supply-chain dimension attaches to importing states and firms — scored under those actors when entity-specific evidence reaches the proposal threshold — and not against the DPRK floor under no-double-count discipline.

topSignals.title: Uganda 20.3 — Ebola Cross-Border Into Kampala and Wakiso; WHO/MOH Contact-Tracing Active; Boundary at 0.3 Points Above Critical
topSignals.description: Uganda's Ebola Bundibugyo cross-border importation from DRC continues to spread into Kampala and Wakiso districts, with case counts rising from 19 as of June 6. All cases remain traceable to DRC travel; community transmission has not yet been ruled out. WHO and MOH contact-tracing is active. Uganda's 20.3 composite sits 0.3 points above Critical. The natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count ruling holds: the cross-border importation represents exogenous epidemiological spread and Uganda's documented cooperation with WHO PHEIC response distinguishes this case from deliberate obstruction. The conversion trigger — documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response including access denial, fund diversion, or case-data suppression — has not been met. Sustained Kampala community transmission with documented state-response failure would move toward Critical; deliberate obstruction would move toward the active-perpetration floor.

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 — 124th Consecutive Week of No-to-Executions Campaign; 40+ Political Executions Since February 28; Near-Floor Confirmed
topSignals.description: Iran's 124th consecutive week of the No-to-Executions campaign — sustained across 56 prison facilities — documents the most durable organized prisoner-rights resistance campaign in the current assessment universe. Two Kurdish political prisoners were executed June 1; 40+ political executions since February 28 constitute the highest documented rate of political executions since the metric was first tracked for this entity. The 784+ YTD total translates to approximately one execution every 44 hours in 2026. The single-dominant-extremity hold at near-floor 2.5 is maintained: the multi-category absolute-0.0 threshold requires a second dominant harm category at comparable scale to the execution rate. No such second category was documented this cycle. Confirmed at 2.5 Absolute Critical.

topSignals.title: Anthropic 59.1 — Amodei Governance Push, $350M Pledge, Advanced AI Framework, IPO S-1 All Resolve Net-Neutral; Confirmed Functional
topSignals.description: Anthropic's June 11 governance cluster — Dario Amodei's publicly stated push for government authority to block the deployment of unsafe models, a $350M pledge ($200M Economic Futures Fund and $150M fellowship program), publication of the Advanced AI Framework proposing mandatory third-party safety testing and revenue-tied civil penalties, and the IPO S-1 filing at approximately $965B valuation — is resolved as corporate-governance-posture net-neutral. Prospective governance posture does not equal enacted governance change; a capital-markets filing is a corporate-structure event; third-party testing frameworks are proposals, not compliance documentation; and the Great American AI Act (June 4 discussion draft) is proposed legislation, not an enacted compliance obligation. None constitutes an operative compassion-conduct change. Confirmed at 59.1 Functional. The DC Circuit ruling (Anthropic v. Hegseth), EU AI Act GPAI compliance (August 2, 52 days), and enacted Great American AI Act compliance documentation remain the operative scoring pivots.

topSignals.title: United States 17.5 — FL 287(g) Arrests at 39k/416 Days All 67 Counties; ICE Ends Detainee-Death Reporting June 4; Priced-Continuation Confirmed
topSignals.description: Florida's 287(g) immigration enforcement has produced 39,000 arrests across 416 days with all 67 counties participating — the broadest geographic coverage of any state-level federal immigration enforcement program in documented history. ICE's June 4 decision to end the post-release detainee-death reporting requirement eliminates the primary data infrastructure for tracking continued death accumulation; 17 in-custody deaths YTD remains the highest per-detention rate in more than two decades. The June 9 GAO Fort Bliss report — three deaths including a destroyed-evidence homicide and $18M wasted — carries forward as an accountability-deficit anchor. The HUD abandonment of housing-first policy deepens the SYS-dimension record. All evidence is same-regime-continuation at the applied 17.5 Critical score; no new un-priced multi-category floor harm was documented this cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Adverse adjudication on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory in the Washington state class action — the remaining active civil adjudication channel after WA and MO state investigations closed without resolution on June 11. Executed terminations are in force; the legal theory is unresolved. Adverse class-action ruling or future regulatory settlement constitutes an immediate scoring trigger from 0.6 points above Critical.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical entering the post-deadline phase. The June 15 sign-or-forfeit window has passed and approximately 30,000 terminations are executed; the WA and MO investigations closed with no adverse ruling. The Washington state class action on the WARN-avoidance theory is the active remaining adjudication channel. The zero-net-payout structure for cancer patients, pregnant employees, H-1B holders, veterans, and RSU-milestone workers constitutes the EMP-dimension record accumulating against the boundary.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO Ebola response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — constitutes the conversion trigger from the natural-disaster-overlay hold. Sustained Kampala community transmission with documented state-response failure would move toward Critical. Deliberate obstruction would move toward the active-perpetration floor.
boundaryWatch.note: Uganda at 0.3 points above Critical as Ebola Bundibugyo continues to spread into Kampala and Wakiso with rising case counts from the June 6 baseline of 19. All cases traceable to DRC travel; community transmission not yet ruled out. WHO/MOH contact-tracing active; Uganda's cooperative engagement with WHO PHEIC response maintains the natural-disaster-overlay hold through the July peak window.

boundaryWatch.trigger: ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025). Verified cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers is the primary reversal signal. The RSF terrorist designation this cycle creates a new legal dimension: verified continued UAE arms or financial transfers to a designated terrorist organization would constitute a qualitatively different complicity category. Documented direct UAE-force participation in Sudan moves toward floor assessment.
boundaryWatch.note: UAE at its applied 18.4 Critical score following the June 8 band crossing. The June 11 RSF terrorist designation adds a new accountability dimension to the existing active-complicity-by-proxy record: any verified continued transfers to the RSF after the designation would fall under a new legal category. ICJ case unadjudicated; applied score is the operative record; no UAE-specific evidence in the June 11 scanner cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law. Documented mass-casualty event under the signed law attributable to state forces constitutes a new harm category scorable beyond 18.4. The June 8 military-crackdown law and 10 killed / 365+ arrested are the events the applied downgrade from 28.4 encoded; ILO/HRW Convention-87 condemnation deepens the international-accountability record.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia at its applied 18.4 Critical score entering the 10th consecutive week of protest activity. June 11 military crackdown law and ILO/HRW Convention-87 condemnation are same-regime-continuation of the priced 18.4 CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION evidence base; no new impunity-law event this cycle.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Appellate review of the Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement would be the first upward-direction indicator within the applied 17.5 Critical baseline. Continued non-reinstatement deepens the transparency-deficit record each cycle.
boundaryWatch.note: United States at its applied 17.5 Critical score. June 11 FL 287(g) surge (39k/416d, all 67 counties), ICE death-reporting rescission (June 4), and GAO Fort Bliss continuation are same-regime continuation at the priced ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 floor — no new un-priced harm category.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle's WARN-avoidance theory remains in legal limbo: the WA and MO state investigations closed without resolution, but the Washington state class action is still an active civil adjudication channel. Each passing cycle without adjudication extends the documentation record at 0.6 points above Critical.
emergingRisks.detail: The post-deadline ruling — executed-but-unadjudicated terminations do not verify the WARN-avoidance theory — is methodologically significant. The Washington state class action is now the primary remaining legal pathway. Adverse class-action adjudication or future regulatory settlement on the WARN-avoidance theory constitutes an immediate crossing trigger from 0.6 points above Critical into the Critical band.

emergingRisks.risk: Ebola Bundibugyo single-day acceleration to +37 cases tracks with models projecting a late-June surge; DRC reaches 635/127 with 18 health zones and no Bundibugyo vaccine; Uganda at 0.3 above Critical with Kampala spread active.
emergingRisks.detail: The +37 single-day case increase is the highest documented single-cycle jump in this outbreak. Contact-tracing at sub-20% coverage across 18 health zones, combined with no approved Bundibugyo vaccine and Kampala urban spread, creates a compounding risk profile entering the July peak window. Documented state obstruction of WHO access remains the conversion trigger for both entities; the July 31 peak window is the structural review deadline.

emergingRisks.risk: Sudan's RSF terrorist designation creates a new accountability dimension: verified continued UAE arms or financial transfers to a designated terrorist organization would constitute a qualitatively different complicity category than the pre-designation active-complicity-by-proxy record scored in the June 8 band crossing.
emergingRisks.detail: The RSF terrorist designation adds a legal accountability layer to the existing UN Fact-Finding Mission genocide-characteristics finding. Any verified post-designation UAE transfers to the RSF would need to be assessed against the terrorist-organization complicity standard rather than the pre-designation proxy-complicity standard. The ICJ Sudan v. UAE case remains unadjudicated; the designation strengthens the plaintiff's evidentiary position.

emergingRisks.risk: North Korea's EU supply-chain forced-labor documentation reaches the June 14 EU high-risk-database obligation deadline; entity-specific Fortune 500 supply-chain audit findings would be independently scorable on EMP and ACC dimensions for importing firms.
emergingRisks.detail: The June 14 EU obligation is the nearest hard deadline on the importer-side enforcement axis opened by the North Korea forced-labor documentation. No entity-specific Fortune 500 supply-chain findings have crossed the proposal threshold this cycle; the risk is a monitoring signal pending entity-specific audit confirmation. CSDDD audit obligations, once fully in effect, create the scoring infrastructure for entity-level proposals.

emergingRisks.risk: The EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take effect August 2 — 52 days from this cycle — with no assessed AI lab having published required compliance documentation; Anthropic's IPO S-1 does not constitute compliance disclosure.
emergingRisks.detail: Non-compliance fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. The Great American AI Act bipartisan draft adds mandatory twice-yearly audits and $1M/day civil penalties at the domestic level. Both instruments bear on the same compliance-documentation gap. August 2 is the nearer of the two hard deadlines and is now within the 60-day observation window.

emergingRisks.risk: Iran's political execution frequency continues to escalate: 784+ YTD with 40+ political executions since February 28 represents the highest documented rate of political executions since the single-dominant-extremity hold was first applied to the 2.5 composite.
emergingRisks.detail: The single-dominant-extremity hold at near-floor 2.5 is maintained because the multi-category absolute-0.0 threshold requires a second dominant harm category at comparable scale. The accelerating political-execution frequency deepens the documentation record against the single-category 2.5 floor. A documented second dominant harm category — mass detention of a distinct minority group at scale, forced disappearances documented at scale — would break the hold and trigger a reassessment toward the absolute floor.

emergingRisks.risk: Bolivia at 18.4 Critical entering the 10th consecutive week of protest activity under the presumption-of-legality military-force law; documented mass-casualty event under the signed law attributable to state forces would constitute a new harm category beyond the priced 18.4 baseline.
emergingRisks.detail: Nine consecutive weeks of military deployment under the codified-impunity law have established a durable pattern without a single mass-casualty event that would trigger re-proposal. The ILO/HRW Convention-87 condemnation deepens the international accountability record. The tenth week represents the longest uninterrupted deployment under the law; the pattern duration itself is now a monitoring signal independent of any single event.

emergingRisks.risk: The Great American AI Act discussion draft and EU AI Act August 2 deadline are converging regulatory vectors creating a dual-track compliance pressure for the AI-lab index that no assessed lab has yet addressed with published documentation.
emergingRisks.detail: The Great American AI Act (June 4 discussion draft) proposes mandatory safety-framework publication, twice-yearly audits, and $1M/day civil penalties. The EU AI Act GPAI obligations effective August 2 require transparency and systematic-risk documentation. Both instruments bear on the same documentation gap. Compliance action — published documentation, submitted frameworks, or formal regulatory engagement — would be the first positive AI-governance signal in the current cycle sequence.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Active-Perpetration Floor Cohort: Four Floors Reinforced With Acute June 11 Evidence
sectorAlerts.headline: Israel (218/243 ceasefire days with attacks, 70%-seizure advancing), Myanmar (cluster-munition school attack, 6 killed), Sudan (RSF terrorist designation, 28.9M food insecure), and North Korea (100k+ transnational forced labor in EU supply chains) each absorb new evidence deepening but not moving the 0.0 floor
observations: Israel 0.0: 218 of 243 ceasefire days with documented attacks; 36% of allocated aid trucks entering; 3,076+ ceasefire violations documented; Oxfam 100+ days water deprivation; 70%-of-Gaza seizure plan advancing with 40 military outpost positions — structural-permanence marker now extends over multiple assessment cycles

Myanmar 0.0: June 9 cluster-munition school attack in Karen State kills 6, establishing the use of internationally-prohibited munitions against educational infrastructure as a documented June event; June 9 airstrike on Karenni youth leader; June 4 Kachin hospital hit — three protected-site attack events in eight days across three geographic theaters

Sudan 0.0: RSF terrorist designation adds new legal accountability dimension to existing genocide-characteristics finding; el-Fasher detention killings documented by Foreign Policy June 11; 28.9M acutely food insecure with the $2.8B appeal only 40% funded represents the most severe compound emergency in the current universe by absolute scale

North Korea 0.0: transnational forced-labor documentation spans 40 countries and is now confirmed in EU supply chains; EU high-risk-database obligation due June 14 is the nearest enforcement trigger; importer-side dimension scored under importing states and Fortune 500 firms — no double-count against DPRK floor

No double-counting across external complicity actors: UAE (Sudan/RSF arming) scored under UAE; EU-importer dimension (DPRK supply-chain) scored under importing states/firms; Microsoft Azure/Unit 8200 scored under Microsoft — consistent no-double-count discipline across all four floor entities

sectorAlerts.sector: Fortune 500 — WARN Boundary: Post-Deadline Phase Methodology Ruling
sectorAlerts.headline: Oracle at 0.6 points above Critical enters the post-deadline phase: ~30,000 terminations executed but WA/MO investigations closed without a ruling — new WARN-HELD-POST-DEADLINE ruling establishes that executed terminations without adjudication do not verify the WARN-avoidance theory
observations: Oracle 20.6: The June 15 sign-or-forfeit deadline has passed — executed terminations are in force: workers signed legal releases waiving the right to sue or forfeited severance; WARN 60-day pay absorbed into severance; RSUs forfeited without acceleration; H-1B/cancer/pregnant/veteran workers disproportionately affected

The new WARN-HELD-POST-DEADLINE-BUT-STILL-PRE-ADJUDICATION ruling establishes precedent: the trigger condition was an adjudication, not mere execution; the WA and MO investigations closed with no adverse ruling; executed-but-unadjudicated does not verify un-priced harm; hold maintained at boundary 20.6

The Washington state class action remains the active civil adjudication channel after investigation closures; an adverse ruling on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is the conversion trigger from 0.6 above Critical into the Critical band

The zero-net-payout structure for excluded workers accumulates EMP-dimension documentation with each passing cycle; the post-deadline phase extends the boundary watch into an indefinite adjudication-pending window

sectorAlerts.sector: Ebola PHEIC — Single-Day +37 Case Surge; Peak Window Approaching
sectorAlerts.headline: DRC Ebola Bundibugyo reaches 635/127 with a +37 single-day case increase — the highest documented single-cycle jump in this outbreak — as 18 Ituri health zones are affected and Uganda's Kampala spread continues; July peak window is the structural review deadline for both entities
observations: DRC 2.3: 635 confirmed cases / 127 deaths from 598/115 in the prior cycle — +37 cases and +12 deaths in approximately 24 hours is the single-day surge peak of the outbreak; 18 of 36 Ituri health zones now affected; contact-tracing at sub-20% coverage

No approved Bundibugyo-strain vaccine exists, distinguishing this outbreak from prior Zaire-strain responses where ring vaccination provided rapid containment; the intervention toolkit is limited to contact-tracing, isolation, and supportive care — all resource-intensive in a conflict-affected zone

Uganda 20.3: Cross-border spread into Kampala and Wakiso with cases rising from the June 6 baseline of 19; all cases traceable to DRC travel; community transmission not yet ruled out; WHO/MOH contact-tracing active

Natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count holds for both entities: primary harm driver is exogenous epidemiological spread, not deliberate state action; cooperative government engagement with WHO PHEIC response is documented for both DRC and Uganda; deliberate obstruction remains the only conversion trigger

The July 31 peak window is the structural review deadline; the +37 single-day acceleration suggests the peak may arrive earlier than July 31 — both entities are elevated to daily monitoring priority through the peak

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Governance Cluster Net-Neutral; August 2 EU AI Act Deadline at 52 Days
sectorAlerts.headline: Anthropic's June 11 governance cluster — Amodei push to block unsafe models, $350M pledge, Advanced AI Framework, IPO S-1 at ~$965B — resolves as net-neutral under the CORPORATE-GOVERNANCE-POSTURE ruling; EU AI Act GPAI obligations at 52 days remain the operative lab-sector scoring pivot
observations: Anthropic 59.1: The June 11 cluster is the most concentrated single-day governance-posture event for any AI lab in the current cycle sequence; all four elements (government-authority push, $350M pledge, Advanced AI Framework, IPO S-1) resolved as prospective posture or corporate-structure events — no operative conduct change

The CORPORATE-GOVERNANCE-POSTURE-NET-NEUTRAL ruling pattern now covers IPO S-1 filings (first applied June 10), Advanced AI Framework proposals (first applied June 11), and philanthropic pledges tied to governance advocacy — extending the principle that prospective posture does not equal enacted governance change

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations effective August 2 — 52 days — represent the nearest hard compliance deadline; no assessed AI lab has published required documentation; Anthropic's IPO S-1 does not constitute compliance disclosure

Great American AI Act bipartisan discussion draft (June 4) proposes mandatory safety-framework publication, twice-yearly audits, $1M/day civil penalties — not yet enacted; net-neutral absent passage; if enacted, would produce the first domestically-legislated scoring trigger for the AI-labs index

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth (Pentagon supply-chain risk) remains the lab sector's nearest judicial scoring pivot; favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Priced-Continuation Cluster: US, Bolivia, India Confirmed at Applied Scores
sectorAlerts.headline: United States (17.5), Bolivia (18.4), and India (15.6) confirm at recently applied Critical-band scores on same-regime-continuation evidence; no new un-priced harm categories documented; priced-continuation discipline holds across all three
observations: United States 17.5: FL 287(g) surge to 39,000 arrests across 416 days with all 67 counties participating is the broadest documented state-level immigration enforcement program coverage in the assessment record; ICE June 4 death-reporting rescission removes the primary accountability data infrastructure; GAO Fort Bliss carry-forward

Bolivia 18.4: 10th consecutive week of protest activity under the June 8 military-crackdown law; ILO/HRW Convention-87 condemnation deepens the international accountability record; no new impunity-law event this cycle — CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION evidence base unchanged from the applied score

India 15.6: ~5,000 detect-delete-deport expulsions including Indian Muslim citizens and Rohingya without due process documented by HRW, USCIRF, and UN; deepens the EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile; escalation within pattern at the applied score from June 1

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Near-Floor Cohort: Iran, DRC, Haiti Confirmed
sectorAlerts.headline: Iran (2.5), DRC (2.3), and Haiti (4.7) confirm at published near-floor scores; DRC Ebola +37-case single-day surge is the most kinetically significant change in the near-floor cohort; Iran's 124th-week execution campaign is the most durable sustained-pattern signal
observations: Iran 2.5: 124th consecutive week of the No-to-Executions campaign across 56 prison facilities documents the longest sustained organized prisoner-rights resistance campaign in the current assessment universe; 2 Kurdish political prisoners executed June 1; 40+ political executions since February 28; 784+ YTD

DRC 2.3: The +37 single-day case increase brings Ebola Bundibugyo to 635/127 — among the most acute single-cycle changes in the near-floor cohort this cycle; natural-disaster-overlay holds as WHO response remains unobstructed; deliberate obstruction is the conversion trigger

Haiti 4.7: 30,000 new displacements in 10 days; record 1.5M total IDPs; PaP IDPs exceeded 300,000 for the first time; 110,000+ forced returns documented; UN SG June 8 visit characterized conditions as harrowing; non-state-driven gang-collapse designation maintained — not a new state-harm category

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Forced Labor and Supply-Chain Transparency: June 14 EU Obligation
sectorAlerts.headline: North Korea forced-labor documentation in EU supply chains — 100k+ workers across 40 countries — approaches the June 14 EU high-risk-database obligation; no Fortune 500 entity crossed the proposal threshold this cycle but the importer-side enforcement axis is activated
observations: North Korea 0.0 floor-state generates supply-chain externalities across EU manufacturing via state-organized forced-labor export; documentation now confirmed across electronics, construction materials, and apparel in multiple EU member states

The June 14 EU high-risk-database obligation is the nearest hard enforcement deadline: entity-specific supply-chain audit findings naming Fortune 500 purchasers would be independently scorable on EMP and ACC dimensions

Walmart, Boeing, and Microsoft flagged as monitoring candidates based on EU manufacturing exposure and opaque tier-2/tier-3 supplier relationships; no entity-specific evidence crossed the proposal threshold this cycle

The methodology principle: the DPRK sovereign floor cannot be moved by documentation of outbound forced labor — the floor is constituted by the domestic systematic-oppression record; the scorable axis is the importer-side enforcement question scored under importing states and firms under no-double-count discipline

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Governance — Great American AI Act + EU AI Act: Dual-Track Regulatory Convergence
sectorAlerts.headline: The bipartisan Great American AI Act discussion draft (June 4) and EU AI Act August 2 GPAI deadline represent the most proximate dual-track regulatory pressure on the AI-lab index since the current cycle sequence began; no assessed lab has published compliance documentation for either instrument
observations: Great American AI Act: bipartisan discussion draft at procedural stage; mandatory safety-framework publication, twice-yearly audits, $1M/day civil penalties for non-compliance; not yet enacted; net-neutral absent passage but represents the first domestically-legislated compliance framework proposal for US-based AI labs

EU AI Act GPAI: transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations effective August 2 — 52 days; fines reach 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover; Anthropic's IPO S-1 filing does not constitute compliance documentation

Compliance action — published safety frameworks, submitted systematic-risk documentation, or formal regulatory engagement with either instrument — would be the first positive AI-governance scored signal in the current cycle sequence

The gap between governance posture (announced intentions, pledges, framework proposals) and governance conduct (published documentation, enacted compliance, verifiable behavior change) has been consistent across the AI-labs cohort for multiple cycles — the August 2 deadline begins to close or formalize that gap

methodologyNotes.name: WARN-HELD-POST-DEADLINE-BUT-STILL-PRE-ADJUDICATION (Oracle — first application of post-deadline variant)
methodologyNotes.description: A passed WARN Act deadline with executed terminations does not constitute an adverse adjudication of a WARN-avoidance theory. The June 15 sign-or-forfeit window closure and execution of approximately 30,000 terminations (workers required to sign releases or forfeit severance; WARN pay absorbed into severance; RSUs forfeited without acceleration) document that the termination structure is in force. The Washington state and Missouri investigations closing without a ruling means the contested 60-day-as-severance structuring remains a disputed-but-unadjudicated compliance interpretation — one that courts have historically treated as lawful in many circuits. The hold condition requires an adverse adjudication: a ruling, settlement, or regulatory determination that the structuring constituted unlawful WARN-Act avoidance. Execution alone does not meet this condition. Oracle held at boundary 20.6; Washington state class action remains the active adjudication channel. This is the first application of the post-deadline variant of WARN-HELD; the pre-execution variant was applied in prior cycles.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-PERPETRATION-FLOOR-REINFORCEMENT (Israel, Myanmar, Sudan, North Korea — June 11)
methodologyNotes.description: Four entities confirmed at the absolute 0.0 floor with acute June 11 evidence that deepens but cannot move the designation. Israel: 218/243 ceasefire-day attacks, 36% aid entry, 3,076+ violations, Oxfam water-deprivation documentation, 70%-seizure advancing. Myanmar: June 9 cluster-munition school attack (Karen State, 6 killed), June 9 Karenni youth leader airstrike, June 4 Kachin hospital hit — three protected-site events across three theaters in eight days. Sudan: RSF terrorist designation, 28.9M food insecure, el-Fasher detention killings, $2.8B appeal 40% funded. North Korea: 100,000+ transnational state forced labor across 40 countries, wages to nuclear program, EU supply chains documented. All four floors are fully constituted; evidence accumulates against the accountability record. No-double-count discipline: UAE (Sudan/RSF), Microsoft (Israel/Unit 8200), EU-importer (DPRK supply-chain) scored under those actors.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT — Ebola Bundibugyo Peak Approach (DRC, Uganda)
methodologyNotes.description: The Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak continues to be treated as an exogenous shock under the natural-disaster-overlay-non-double-count ruling. DRC (635/127, +37 in one day, 18 health zones) and Uganda (Kampala/Wakiso spread, cases rising from June 6 baseline) both maintain the overlay: the primary harm driver is an exogenous epidemiological event, and both governments are cooperating with WHO PHEIC response. The +37 single-day acceleration is noted as the outbreak's highest single-cycle case increase; it raises the peak-window monitoring priority but does not by itself constitute deliberate state obstruction. The conversion trigger remains unchanged: documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response — access denial, fund diversion, case-data suppression — by either government breaks the overlay. The July 31 peak window is the structural review deadline.

methodologyNotes.name: CORPORATE-GOVERNANCE-POSTURE-NET-NEUTRAL Extended (Anthropic — Advanced AI Framework and Philanthropic Pledge)
methodologyNotes.description: The CORPORATE-GOVERNANCE-POSTURE-NET-NEUTRAL ruling is extended to cover two new event types in addition to the IPO S-1 (first applied June 10): (1) Advanced AI Framework proposals — voluntary governance frameworks proposing third-party testing and revenue-tied penalties are prospective posture, not enacted compliance obligations; (2) Philanthropic pledges tied to governance advocacy — the $350M pledge ($200M Economic Futures Fund, $150M fellowship) is a capital-allocation decision, not a compassion-conduct change. The Amodei push for government authority to block unsafe models is a policy-advocacy posture position, not an enacted regulatory change. All four June 11 events resolved net-neutral. The principle applies to all AI labs: announced intentions, pledges, and framework proposals are posture; enacted compliance documentation, verifiable behavior change, and operational governance mechanisms are conduct.

methodologyNotes.name: PRICED-CONTINUATION / SAME-REGIME-CONTINUATION (US, Bolivia, India — June 11)
methodologyNotes.description: All three entities at recently applied lower scores; June 11 evidence is same-regime continuation. United States 17.5: FL 287(g) 39k/416d all-67-counties surge and ICE death-reporting rescission deepen the ACC 1.4/INT 1.5 profile; no new un-priced multi-category floor harm. Bolivia 18.4: ILO/HRW Convention-87 condemnation deepens the CODIFIED-IMPUNITY-ESCALATION record; no new impunity-law event; tenth week of protest without a mass-casualty trigger. India 15.6: ~5,000 detect-delete-deport expulsions deepen the EQU 1.3/EMP 1.4/ACC 1.4 profile; escalation within pattern. Re-litigation requires a new un-priced harm category, not deepened documentation of the existing priced pattern.

methodologyNotes.name: COORDINATED-INVESTIGATION-STILL-PRE-ADJUDICATION (UnitedHealth Group — re-confirmed June 11)
methodologyNotes.description: UnitedHealth Group confirmed at 10.2: DOJ Massachusetts criminal Medicare-Advantage risk-score-inflation probe, coordinated multi-AG investigations, active AI claim-denial cases, and shareholder suit constitute rising enforcement density but no merits determination. CEO change (Witty to Hemsley) is governance churn without a compassion-harm signal. Enforcement density is not adjudication; the conversion trigger remains a merits adjudication, settlement, or formal charge on any of the converging enforcement actions.

methodologyNotes.name: SCALE-FLOOR-ADJACENT (Iran) + NON-STATE-DRIVEN-CONTINUATION (Haiti) — June 11
methodologyNotes.description: Iran re-affirmed at floor-adjacent 2.5: 124th-week No-to-Executions campaign, 2 Kurdish political prisoners executed June 1, 40+ political executions since February 28, 784+ YTD — single dominant extremity already priced; no new multi-category 0.0 threshold evidence. Haiti re-affirmed at 4.7: 30,000 new displacements in 10 days, record 1.5M IDPs, PaP IDPs exceeded 300k, 110k+ forced returns — gang-driven collapse is non-state-driven continuation at the near-floor score; not a new state-harm category. Both reconstruct exact.

methodologyNotes.name: ROTATION-CONFIRM (Moldova, Tanzania, Sierra Leone, Dominican Republic, Peru — June 11)
methodologyNotes.description: Five rotation entities confirmed at published 35.9 Developing; scanner evidence_found:false over the 14-day lookback — no acute negative event. Balanced approximately 2.5 baseline profile with EQU 2.0 relative soft spot. All reconstruct exact. Staleness-selected from last_assessed:never cohort; stable baseline expectation confirmed.

forwardTriggers.entity: North Korea / Importing States and Firms
forwardTriggers.trigger: EU high-risk-database obligation due June 14. Entity-specific Fortune 500 supply-chain audit findings naming purchasers with documented North Korean forced-labor exposure would be independently scorable on EMP and ACC dimensions for those firms. The DPRK sovereign floor is unchanged; the importer-side enforcement axis is activated by the obligation deadline.

Ebola Bundibugyo peak window through July. 635 cases / 127 deaths with +37 single-day surge; 18 health zones; no approved Bundibugyo vaccine. Documented deliberate state obstruction of the WHO response breaks the NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at the near-floor 2.3 composite. The +37 single-day acceleration suggests peak may arrive before July 31.

Ebola Kampala and Wakiso urban spread peak window through July. Cases rising from June 6 baseline of 19; community transmission not ruled out. WHO-coordinated response active; documented state obstruction of WHO access is the crossing trigger from 0.3 points above Critical. Sustained community transmission with documented state-response failure moves toward Critical.

EU AI Act GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations take full effect. Fines up to 35 million euros or 7% of global annual turnover. No assessed AI lab has published compliance documentation — 52 days from this cycle. Anthropic IPO S-1 filing does not constitute compliance disclosure.

Washington state class action adverse ruling on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory — the remaining active civil adjudication channel after WA and MO state investigations closed without resolution on June 11. Adverse ruling or future regulatory settlement constitutes an immediate crossing trigger from 0.6 points above Critical into the Critical band.

ICJ ruling in Sudan v. UAE (filed March 2025). The RSF terrorist designation this cycle adds a new accountability dimension: verified continued UAE arms or financial transfers to a designated terrorist organization post-designation would constitute a qualitatively different complicity category. Verified cessation of RSF arms and mercenary transfers is the primary reversal signal for the applied 18.4 Critical composite.

Judicial annulment or legislative repeal of the June 8 presumption-of-legality military-force law. Documented mass-casualty event under the signed law attributable to state forces constitutes a new harm category scorable beyond 18.4. The 10th consecutive week of deployment without a mass-casualty trigger maintains the priced-continuation designation.

Appellate review of Murphy (D. Mass.) and Cobb (D.D.C.) deportation injunctions. Reinstatement of the rescinded June 4 ICE post-release death-reporting requirement is the first upward-direction indicator. Continued non-reinstatement deepens the transparency-deficit record each cycle at the priced 17.5 Critical baseline.

Merits adjudication or settlement of any of the converging sovereign enforcement actions — DOJ MA risk-score criminal probe, coordinated multi-AG investigations, AI claim-denial cases, or shareholder suit. AZ AG adjudication would simultaneously reach multiple other Fortune 500 health insurers.

DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth (Pentagon supply-chain risk). Favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement. IPO completion does not affect this trigger. Enacted Great American AI Act compliance documentation would be independently scorable. EU AI Act non-compliance fines would produce downward movement.

Documented second dominant harm category at comparable scale to the political-execution rate — mass detention of a distinct minority group at scale, or documented forced disappearances at scale — would break the single-dominant-extremity hold at 2.5 and trigger a reassessment toward the absolute floor. Verified halt of political executions is the primary reversal signal.

Documented cessation of systematic civilian targeting across all four documented target categories (schools, hospitals, religious sites, residential infrastructure) and verified accountability mechanism activation. Floor exits through demonstrated perpetration cessation, not through time or evidence accumulation.

IPC Phase 5 famine area expansion; verified ceasefire and humanitarian access restoration; ICC or ICJ accountability proceedings against RSF or SAF leadership. RSF terrorist designation creates a new enforcement axis: sanctions enforcement actions on the RSF financial network and sponsoring states constitute forward signals.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: Does the WA/MO WARN-Act investigation closure without a ruling — leaving Oracle's executed-but-unadjudicated 30,000 terminations in a post-deadline legal limbo — constitute sufficient basis for the Washington state class action to proceed on the remote-reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory, or does the investigation closure signal that adjudicating bodies have assessed the 60-day-as-severance structuring as lawful?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Oracle's boundary hold survives the deadline: executed terminations and closed investigations coexist without an adjudication — the methodology's most precisely calibrated current carry-forward.
